questions.question: What value of the display property makes an element generate a box that is both inline-level and block-level?
questions.explanation: display: inline-block generates an inline-level block container. The element flows inline like text but can have width, height, and vertical margins/padding applied like a block element.

questions.question: What causes unwanted gaps between inline-block elements?
questions.explanation: Whitespace characters (spaces, tabs, newlines) between inline-block elements in the HTML source are rendered as visible gaps. Common fixes include removing whitespace in the source, using negative margins, or setting font-size: 0 on the parent.

questions.question: What is the default vertical-align value for inline-block elements?
questions.explanation: The default vertical-align for inline-block elements is baseline. This means the baseline of the element aligns with the baseline of the parent's text, which can cause unexpected alignment when elements have different content heights.

questions.question: Which is an advantage of using inline-block over float for layout?
choices: Inline-block does not require a clearfix on the parent
Inline-block elements cannot have width set
questions.explanation: Unlike floated elements, inline-block elements remain in the normal document flow, so the parent container automatically expands to contain them without needing a clearfix hack.

questions.question: The ___ property controls how inline-block elements align relative to each other on a line.
questions.explanation: The vertical-align property controls the vertical alignment of inline-block elements relative to the line box. Common values include top, middle, bottom, and baseline.

questions.question: Setting font-size: 0 on a parent element is one technique to remove ___ gaps between inline-block children.
questions.explanation: Setting font-size: 0 on the parent collapses the rendered whitespace between inline-block children to zero width. You must then reset font-size on the children themselves.
